Choose from a range of AI models to power your app with the intelligence it needs.Vertically integrated tech stackLive app preview >> See real-time changes without leaving the workbench.Inline code editing >> Allows builders to see and interact with the underlying code powering their Spark in real time via Codespaces for deeper development.One-click deployment >> Deploy apps directly from Spark with minimal setup.GitHub platform and ecosystemShare your sparks >> Choose to share your sparks privately with specific people—providing added flexibility to manage your sparks.Export to repo >> Save and version your app in a GitHub repository.Auth built-in >> GitHub authentication built-in from day one.

slides.title: App Modernization
slides.notes: While Agent Mode and Coding agent support developers with a variety of tasks in their day-to-day workflows, specialized App Modernization agents make it easier than ever to quickly modernize Java and .NET apps.Developers can quickly upgrade Java or .NET versions and associated frameworks to keep apps running optimally and increase performance.Developers can use agents to perform end-to-end code assessment and remediation to streamline migration of existing apps to Azure.Developers can also use AI agents to auto-remediate code changes and bug fixes in preparation to move them to Azure app platform services.And just like with Coding agents, developers stay in control by providing guidance and applying organizational knowledge and best practices to validate the changes made by agents.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.notes: Thank you

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: Copilot Studio empowers organizations to go beyond product copilots by creating custom AI agents.With a simple visual interface, it delivers automation, intelligent conversations, and business impact.It helps solve real-world challenges by combining AI, data, and workflows in one place

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Every company’s experience will be different, but we see this journey to a hybrid team of humans and agents playing out in three phases, with each phase increasing the ability to scale rapidly, operate with agility, and generate value faster.In the first phase, AI acts as an assistant, helping people do the same work better and faster—such as retrieving information.In phase 2, agents join teams as “digital colleagues,” taking on specific tasks at human direction—for example, someone can ask a researcher agent to create a go-to-market plan.And in phase 3—people set direction for agents that run entire business processes and workflows, checking in as needed.The journey to the Frontier Firm is not a strictly linear progression—in many cases, organizations will be in all three phases simultaneously.

slides.title: What are agents?
slides.notes: So what are agents?Agents exist on a spectrum. Different organizations are at different stages of their AI transformation. Some are advanced and building the next generation of agents with autonomous capabilities. Others are still building out their AI agents to focus on getting information and completing tasks.  In 2025 agents are mostly associated with the right side of autonomy. However, we believe agents live on a spectrum of capabilities:Retrieval: Designed with specific instructions on how to behave, their personality, and their overall objectives. They follow predefined rules and guidelines set by the creators.Task: connected to specific workflows or processes. They operate based on knowledge, skills, and rules based automation. Their primary purpose is to automate repetitive tasks.Autonomous: Operate independently, dynamically planning and learning from the processes. They adapt to changing conditions and make decisions without human intervention.

slides.title: Customize your agent
slides.notes: Build any agent. From simple to sophisticated.These are the essential building blocks that make up a custom agent that we discussed. You can tailor these building blocks with Copilot Studio to meet your unique business needs.At the bottom we have a foundation model, but that’s only one piece of the puzzleYou have to add your existing knowledge that your agent can reference, like files or public websitesYou also have to add tools, which are actions your agent can perform using your data and line of business services and appsOrchestrator: The orchestrator runs across your enterprise data and across M365 and Azure. It integrates your company’s knowledge and tools with the foundation models so agents are providing the most personalized, accurate experience to your organization. And there’s the user experience – channels where your end users can engage your agent, like Teams, Facebook or your company’s website. And analytics on how users are interacting with your agentLet’s take a closer look at what you can do with each of these building blocks.

slides.title: Knowledge
slides.notes: Knowledge: Existing enterprise resources that your agent can query – you can see supported data sources hereYou can add your public and enterprise data sources using agent connectors in a few clicks. You can upload files, use third-party connectors, or public websites.Your agent will be able to provide multi-turn answers in real time using your enterprise data.This lets you create an immediately useful agent.

slides.title: Tools
slides.notes: Tools:You can easily connect to your key line of business systems, whether they’re first or third party.Enable your agent to automate your business processes and complete tasks.You have everything from advanced workflows to autonomous agents that use visionYou can access it all in the “Tools” menu Let’s see some examples

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.notes: MCP is the first standardized open protocol made for AI. It simplifies how AI models and agents interact with external data and tools. MCP facilitates seamless interoperability among AI tools by enabling effective communication between diverse AI systems. By adding MCP in Copilot Studio, users can easily connect their own data sources and tools into the platform. That way, they don’t need to spend time creating custom connectors and can build and scale their agents faster.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: We also have agent flows. Agent flows are workflows designed to execute highly structured tasks at scale. They are built to handle predictable and repetitive scenarios like document processing, routine financial approvals, and compliance tasks with impressive speed and accuracy. Agent flows and agents combine to automate complex and mission critical processes. BENEFITSExecutes quickly without requiring AI reasoning at every stepAdd the same agent flow to multiple agents for consistent execution of structured workloads across the businessAdd intelligent steps to manage complex decision points involving conditions and exceptions, or simplify complex operations like loops, data processing, or prediction and analysisAny one can build agent flows with natural language instructions or a graphical user interfaceEXAMPLEIf an organization receives customer feedback, an agent flow can help direct that feedback to the right team based on static rules and policies. If it is general feedback, the agent flow can hand off to a “feedback tracking agent” that can then summarize and generate action items for the team. If it is urgent or negative feedback, the agent flow could hand off to a “customer service agent” that can answer a customer’s question and take action to resolve it.

slides.title: Slide 9
slides.notes: The computer use tool is a new feature in Copilot Studio that lets agents interact with both websites and desktop apps by clicking buttons, selecting menus, and typing fields in a screen. With AI vision and understanding, customers can automate processes on user interfaces for a broad range of tasks from data transfer and intelligent document processing, to more ambiguous tasks like research and analysis. A single task can be performed on multiple virtual machines, meaning you can perform these enterprise automation scenarios at scale. These machines can be hosted by the customer, or on Microsoft hosted machines to accelerate deployment, simplify management, and reduce costs.Takeaway: We have the full spectrum of tools, whether you want something closer to traditional automation or the most cutting edge technology.

slides.title: Orchestrator
slides.notes: The orchestrator ensures that it’s not only the model that is powering your agent, but the model combined with the right enterprise data across systemsLike we saw with agent flows, you can have more defined paths for your agent to follow so you can have complete control over critical scenarios, like HR or legal questions, by designing specific step-by step topics.You can also enable your agent to automatically select the most appropriate action or topic to respond to a user using generative AI so that it can be flexibleEasily mix and manage both defined pathways and generative orchestration in one system

slides.title: Security and Governance
slides.notes: Security and GovernanceThis is the foundation of responsible Copilot adoption.We begin by preparing the environment to support secure deployment—this includes identifying risks related to data, users, and agents to prevent sensitive data leakage.We implement controls to prevent data loss and insider risks, securing sensitive files and agent interactions, while setting up alerts and reports for risky behavior.Governance also means ensuring compliance. We inspect interaction content and audit logs, investigate for ethical or regulatory violations, and enforce lifecycle policies and legal holds.

slides.title: Slide 12
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: AI Development and Agentic DevOps
slides.notes: The role of AI in software development is rapidly expanding, and we’re excited to tell you about our vision for a software lifecycle powered by AI.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Part of the problem is that modern app development is made up more of non-coding tasks than writing new code or even fixing existing code.According to Gartner, up to 60% of a developer’s time is spent on tasks like design, architecture, planning, research, and evaluation. Only 30% of a developer’s time is spent working on existing code, while writing new code only accounts for 10% of a developer’s time.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Our vision for Agentic DevOps is one in which AI-powered agents operate as a member of your dev and ops teams, automating, optimizing, and accelerating every stage of the software lifecycle.We see a future in which autonomous and semi-autonomous agents work together to help:Solve routine and complex tasksBring apps to market fasterIncrease code quality and securityRemove repetitive development tasksReduce technical debtReframe the economics of operating, andMaintain and modernize apps in production.We believe that through agentic DevOps, developers will be able to orchestrate a series of agentic services, with the freedom to focus on higher value creative work, while operators can proactively identity, mitigate and resolve issues in production.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: As apps become more complex, agentic DevOps is emerging as a transformative new approach to software development that is supporting AI innovation. And Microsoft is uniquely positioned to offer a comprehensive platform to realize these benefits.GitHub Copilot provides developers with an advanced, AI-native development experience that builds AI-native capabilities directly into the tools they know and love.But Copilot benefits don’t stop in the IDE—the benefits of AI and Copilot are infused into every stage of the software lifecycle, helping accelerate development and optimize workflows.And as AI becomes ubiquitous across industries, we are committed to making Azure and GitHub the premier platform for building AI applications—taking the complexity out of AI development workflows.Now, let’s take a closer look at the capabilities in GitHub Copilot, Azure, and GitHub that are unlocking this next evolution in DevOps.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: Developers are the heart of software development—and by building agentic capabilities into familiar tools they already know and love, we can redefine the development experience and usher in a new era of developer satisfaction and productivity.From advanced capabilities directly in the IDE, to customizations and extensions that make it easy to tailor AI to your developer’s needs, AI can fundamentally change how developers work to make development easier and more intuitive. AI can even help take an idea from a concept to a functioning app faster by making it easy for developers to get ideas off the ground using natural language and turn them into code they can then refine

slides.title: GitHub Copilot is built for developer experience
slides.notes: GitHub is emerging as the driving force behind this evolution in developer experience.We are building GitHub for the developer experience, helping automate away mundane tasks and empowering developers to focus on what matters.From writing code, tests, and documentation, to fixing bugs, debugging code, and reviewing code, to modernizing code, upgrading dependencies, and updating stakeholders, we are committed to building Copilot to be able to help developers with whatever task they’re facing…

slides.title: Agent Mode
slides.notes: For instance, GitHub Copilot agent mode supercharges the AI-assisted coding experience to become a true peer programmer. Agent mode expands your AI capabilities beyond simple autocomplete, enabling developers to assign multi-step coding tasks to Copilot directly from their IDE.Developers can leverage an AI-powered peer programmer to build new features, refactor legacy code, and heal itself when things break.Agent mode makes it easy to analyze a codebase, edit files, run tests, fix errors, and suggest terminal commands—all from a single prompt.GitHub Copilot Agent mode is available today in Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code, and we are happy to announce public preview support for Xcode, Eclipse, and JetBrains.

slides.title: GitHub Spark
slides.notes: But AI benefits aren’t limited to Copilot—GitHub Spark is an integrated, AI-native development platform that extends GitHub Copilot—combining natural language editing, visual interfaces, and seamless deployment to eliminate the seams between prototyping to production—so every builder can focus on what matters most: bringing their vision to life.AI “out of the box”: Instant app generation with natural language >> Turn ideas into working prototypes using natural language inputs (chat), reducing the barrier between concept and creation.AI-powered app iteration >> Quickly refine apps with AI-assisted tools for logic, data, design, and assets.AI built-In >> Build smarter apps with AI and agentic capabilities built in—no AI expertise needed. Spark works out of the box, and when you dive into code, GitHub Copilot delivers real-time suggestions and autofixes. Choose from a range of AI models to power your app with the intelligence it needs.Vertically integrated tech stackLive app preview >> See real-time changes without leaving the workbench.Inline code editing >> Allows builders to see and interact with the underlying code powering their Spark in real time via Codespaces for deeper development.One-click deployment >> Deploy apps directly from Spark with minimal setup.GitHub platform and ecosystemShare your sparks >> Choose to share your sparks privately with specific people—providing added flexibility to manage your sparks.Export to repo >> Save and version your app in a GitHub repository.Auth built-in >> GitHub authentication built-in from day one.

slides.title: App Modernization
slides.notes: While Agent Mode and Coding agent support developers with a variety of tasks in their day-to-day workflows, specialized App Modernization agents make it easier than ever to quickly modernize Java and .NET apps.Developers can quickly upgrade Java or .NET versions and associated frameworks to keep apps running optimally and increase performance.Developers can use agents to perform end-to-end code assessment and remediation to streamline migration of existing apps to Azure.Developers can also use AI agents to auto-remediate code changes and bug fixes in preparation to move them to Azure app platform services.And just like with Coding agents, developers stay in control by providing guidance and applying organizational knowledge and best practices to validate the changes made by agents.

We inspect interaction content and audit logs, investigate for ethical or regulatory violations, and enforce lifecycle policies and legal holds.

slides.title: Power your digital transformationwith Microsoft for Healthcare | Pharma
slides.notes: Today we’ll explore how pharma companies can power their digital transformation with Microsoft. We’ll start with industry trends and challenges, then walk through how Microsoft can help accelerate research, empower your workforce, enhance patient and provider experiences, operate with agility, and safeguard data and infrastructure. Finally, we’ll touch on trust and next steps.

slides.title: Despite the recent growth trajectory, the Pharma sector is facing major headwinds
slides.notes: In 2025, pharma continues to face mounting pressure in a post-pandemic world. Returns on R&D investment remain at historic lows, with ROI having dropped to just 1.2% in 2022 — the weakest in over a decade of Deloitte’s tracking. The decline was accelerated by the exceptional returns of COVID-19 assets in 2021, which have since normalized. Even without those assets, the falloff is clear — 2021 performance was still nearly double that of 2022 projections.At the same time, average peak sales per asset have slipped from $500M in 2021 to $389M in 2022, signaling shrinking revenue expectations for new launches.By 2025, these trends have left the life sciences sector navigating longer timelines, higher development costs, and lower commercial returns. The imperative is clear: pharma must reinvent how it discovers, develops, and delivers medicines — with digital, data, and AI at the core to restore sustainable growth.

slides.title: Healthcare’s rapid evolution is raising challenges for Pharma
slides.notes: Our mission in Healthcare is to help solve some of the biggest challenges for pharma organizations that are emerging out of today’s rapid industry evolution. Over the last few years, healthcare organizations have been burdened by an unprecedented volume of challenging moments that have forced many to rethink established norms and operational best practices. Developing a new drug costs $1–2.6 Billion and takes 10–15 years from discovery to market153% of companies generating more than $1 Billion in revenue are reporting that silos derail efficiency2Less than 20% of pharmaceutical executives have reported successfully scaling digital and AI transformation3Pharma organizations are experiencing these challenges while also facing pressure to remain secure and compliant with ever-changing regulations. Our commitment has always been to ensure the tools we provide are up to the task of supporting our customers in their time of need. We know that technology has a role to play in accelerating progress for pressing healthcare industry concerns.

slides.title: Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation
slides.notes: Additionally, Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation:Demonstrating product efficacy as medtech companies produce a majority of health data (such as imaging or telemetry), putting pressure on med device companies to optimize products for success in value-based care models. Both med device and medtech companies are looking to develop new digital products to monetize data, algorithms, and workflows, thus changing their role in healthcare (from supplier/enabler to active participant) and the broader life sciences ecosystem. This is also an opportunity to increase brand/franchise affinity.Due to cost sensitivity, customers are frequently opting for new pricing structures and options to bundle medical devices and technologies, vs. traditional buying behaviors of stand-alone purchases, putting pressure on medical device companies to rethink their pricing model.Dashboards are often populated with non-real time data, making it challenging to visualize patient data to execute decisions for better care.Disparity between medical device demand and production, as demand for medical devices have increased and device manufacturers are often unable to get the materials they need to meet that demand.Demonstrating efficacy of medical devices to payors. With the shift to value-based care, many medical devices exist outside traditional insurance coverage because efficacy is not clearly proven.Changing, stringent regulations & long, expensive, and cumbersome FDA evaluation and approval process makes it difficult to seek approval for new developments, discouraging innovation, even as innovation becomes more essential to differentiate in a crowded market.

slides.title: Microsoft is helping Pharma companies like you achieve more
slides.notes: With tools infused across the entire healthcare ecosystem and now with the help of integrated AI, Microsoft is committed to helping you achieve more. slides.title: Healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
slides.notes: The release for healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric includes the following solutions/capabilities:FHIR data ingestion: Bring your Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) data to OneLake from a FHIR service such as Azure Health Data Services. OneLake, in Microsoft Fabric, is a unified, logical data lake that is designed to be a single place for all your analytics data.Healthcare data foundations: Set up your healthcare data estate quickly with FHIR data standards and ready-to-run data pipelines that are designed to efficiently structure data for analytics and AI/machine learning modeling..Unstructured clinical notes enrichment: Utilize Azure AI Language's Text Analytics for health services to extract and add structure to unstructured clinical notes for analytics. OMOP analytics: Prepare data for standardized analytics through Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership (OMOP) open community standards. Dynamics 365 Customer Insights - Data preparation: Connect Dynamics 365 Customer Insights to your OneLake on Fabric for creating patient or member lists for outreach.

slides.title: Azure AI Services for Health
slides.notes: Here is how Azure AI services for health can aid your business through a more healthcare-focused lens.Through Azure, pre-built healthcare AI models enable customers to build their own AI-based applications in the era of GenAI.Here is some information about this service:Azure AI Services for Health leverage Microsoft’s breadth of industry-agnostic AI capabilities. These services are specifically designed for the healthcare domain.Azure AI Services for Health bring pre-built AI tools to developers and data scientists, streamlining the integration of artificial intelligence into health-related applications.These services are created with health-specific models, aiming to enable a variety of use cases within the healthcare industry.The platform extends horizontal services while also adding domain-specific ones where needed, ensuring a tailored approach to healthcare challenges.Customers of all skill levels can easily incorporate AI capabilities into their applications, democratizing access to cutting-edge technology.These services are available via API calls, allowing developers to embed functionalities that enable applications to see, hear, speak, search, understand complex data, and accelerate advanced decision-making.Some Azure AI Services for Health are built on the Cognitive Services platform, while others are standalone offerings designed specifically for healthcare applications.Here are some more specific features that Azure AI services for health can provide, in combination with Azure AI Health Bot with generative AI technology.-Text Analytics for health, Clinical Data Classification, DE-ID, and Medical Vision as a Service Vision for HealthAdditionally, Azure AI services enables:-Azure AI Bot Service, Azure Open AI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure Form Recognizer, Azure Video Analyzer, Azure AI Vision, Azure AI Speech, Azure AI Language

slides.title: Thank you!
slides.notes: Thank you

slides.title: Power your digital transformationwith Microsoft for Healthcare | Pharma
slides.notes: Today we’ll explore how pharma companies can power their digital transformation with Microsoft. We’ll start with industry trends and challenges, then walk through how Microsoft can help accelerate research, empower your workforce, enhance patient and provider experiences, operate with agility, and safeguard data and infrastructure. Finally, we’ll touch on trust and next steps.

slides.title: Healthcare’s rapid evolution is raising challenges for Pharma
slides.notes: Our mission in Healthcare is to help solve some of the biggest challenges for pharma organizations that are emerging out of today’s rapid industry evolution. Over the last few years, healthcare organizations have been burdened by an unprecedented volume of challenging moments that have forced many to rethink established norms and operational best practices. Developing a new drug costs $1–2.6 Billion and takes 10–15 years from discovery to market153% of companies generating more than $1 Billion in revenue are reporting that silos derail efficiency2Less than 20% of pharmaceutical executives have reported successfully scaling digital and AI transformation3Pharma organizations are experiencing these challenges while also facing pressure to remain secure and compliant with ever-changing regulations. Our commitment has always been to ensure the tools we provide are up to the task of supporting our customers in their time of need. We know that technology has a role to play in accelerating progress for pressing healthcare industry concerns.

slides.title: Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation
slides.notes: Additionally, Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation:Demonstrating product efficacy as medtech companies produce a majority of health data (such as imaging or telemetry), putting pressure on med device companies to optimize products for success in value-based care models. Both med device and medtech companies are looking to develop new digital products to monetize data, algorithms, and workflows, thus changing their role in healthcare (from supplier/enabler to active participant) and the broader life sciences ecosystem. This is also an opportunity to increase brand/franchise affinity.Due to cost sensitivity, customers are frequently opting for new pricing structures and options to bundle medical devices and technologies, vs. traditional buying behaviors of stand-alone purchases, putting pressure on medical device companies to rethink their pricing model.Dashboards are often populated with non-real time data, making it challenging to visualize patient data to execute decisions for better care.Disparity between medical device demand and production, as demand for medical devices have increased and device manufacturers are often unable to get the materials they need to meet that demand.Demonstrating efficacy of medical devices to payors. With the shift to value-based care, many medical devices exist outside traditional insurance coverage because efficacy is not clearly proven.Changing, stringent regulations & long, expensive, and cumbersome FDA evaluation and approval process makes it difficult to seek approval for new developments, discouraging innovation, even as innovation becomes more essential to differentiate in a crowded market.

slides.title: AI Development and Agentic DevOps
slides.notes: The role of AI in software development is rapidly expanding, and we’re excited to tell you about our vision for a software lifecycle powered by AI.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Part of the problem is that modern app development is made up more of non-coding tasks than writing new code or even fixing existing code.According to Gartner, up to 60% of a developer’s time is spent on tasks like design, architecture, planning, research, and evaluation. Only 30% of a developer’s time is spent working on existing code, while writing new code only accounts for 10% of a developer’s time.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Our vision for Agentic DevOps is one in which AI-powered agents operate as a member of your dev and ops teams, automating, optimizing, and accelerating every stage of the software lifecycle.We see a future in which autonomous and semi-autonomous agents work together to help:Solve routine and complex tasksBring apps to market fasterIncrease code quality and securityRemove repetitive development tasksReduce technical debtReframe the economics of operating, andMaintain and modernize apps in production.We believe that through agentic DevOps, developers will be able to orchestrate a series of agentic services, with the freedom to focus on higher value creative work, while operators can proactively identity, mitigate and resolve issues in production.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: As apps become more complex, agentic DevOps is emerging as a transformative new approach to software development that is supporting AI innovation. And Microsoft is uniquely positioned to offer a comprehensive platform to realize these benefits.GitHub Copilot provides developers with an advanced, AI-native development experience that builds AI-native capabilities directly into the tools they know and love.But Copilot benefits don’t stop in the IDE—the benefits of AI and Copilot are infused into every stage of the software lifecycle, helping accelerate development and optimize workflows.And as AI becomes ubiquitous across industries, we are committed to making Azure and GitHub the premier platform for building AI applications—taking the complexity out of AI development workflows.Now, let’s take a closer look at the capabilities in GitHub Copilot, Azure, and GitHub that are unlocking this next evolution in DevOps.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: Developers are the heart of software development—and by building agentic capabilities into familiar tools they already know and love, we can redefine the development experience and usher in a new era of developer satisfaction and productivity.From advanced capabilities directly in the IDE, to customizations and extensions that make it easy to tailor AI to your developer’s needs, AI can fundamentally change how developers work to make development easier and more intuitive. AI can even help take an idea from a concept to a functioning app faster by making it easy for developers to get ideas off the ground using natural language and turn them into code they can then refine

slides.title: GitHub Copilot is built for developer experience
slides.notes: GitHub is emerging as the driving force behind this evolution in developer experience.We are building GitHub for the developer experience, helping automate away mundane tasks and empowering developers to focus on what matters.From writing code, tests, and documentation, to fixing bugs, debugging code, and reviewing code, to modernizing code, upgrading dependencies, and updating stakeholders, we are committed to building Copilot to be able to help developers with whatever task they’re facing…

slides.title: Agent Mode
slides.notes: For instance, GitHub Copilot agent mode supercharges the AI-assisted coding experience to become a true peer programmer. Agent mode expands your AI capabilities beyond simple autocomplete, enabling developers to assign multi-step coding tasks to Copilot directly from their IDE.Developers can leverage an AI-powered peer programmer to build new features, refactor legacy code, and heal itself when things break.Agent mode makes it easy to analyze a codebase, edit files, run tests, fix errors, and suggest terminal commands—all from a single prompt.GitHub Copilot Agent mode is available today in Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code, and we are happy to announce public preview support for Xcode, Eclipse, and JetBrains.

slides.title: GitHub Spark
slides.notes: But AI benefits aren’t limited to Copilot—GitHub Spark is an integrated, AI-native development platform that extends GitHub Copilot—combining natural language editing, visual interfaces, and seamless deployment to eliminate the seams between prototyping to production—so every builder can focus on what matters most: bringing their vision to life.AI “out of the box”: Instant app generation with natural language >> Turn ideas into working prototypes using natural language inputs (chat), reducing the barrier between concept and creation.AI-powered app iteration >> Quickly refine apps with AI-assisted tools for logic, data, design, and assets.AI built-In >> Build smarter apps with AI and agentic capabilities built in—no AI expertise needed. Spark works out of the box, and when you dive into code, GitHub Copilot delivers real-time suggestions and autofixes. Choose from a range of AI models to power your app with the intelligence it needs.Vertically integrated tech stackLive app preview >> See real-time changes without leaving the workbench.Inline code editing >> Allows builders to see and interact with the underlying code powering their Spark in real time via Codespaces for deeper development.One-click deployment >> Deploy apps directly from Spark with minimal setup.GitHub platform and ecosystemShare your sparks >> Choose to share your sparks privately with specific people—providing added flexibility to manage your sparks.Export to repo >> Save and version your app in a GitHub repository.Auth built-in >> GitHub authentication built-in from day one.

slides.title: App Modernization
slides.notes: While Agent Mode and Coding agent support developers with a variety of tasks in their day-to-day workflows, specialized App Modernization agents make it easier than ever to quickly modernize Java and .NET apps.Developers can quickly upgrade Java or .NET versions and associated frameworks to keep apps running optimally and increase performance.Developers can use agents to perform end-to-end code assessment and remediation to streamline migration of existing apps to Azure.Developers can also use AI agents to auto-remediate code changes and bug fixes in preparation to move them to Azure app platform services.And just like with Coding agents, developers stay in control by providing guidance and applying organizational knowledge and best practices to validate the changes made by agents.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.notes: Thank you

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry brings together all the elements developers need to harness the potential of AI into a unified, user-friendly, code-first platform.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production.With your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 6
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog now offers nearly 1,600 foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models direct from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI deployments, choosing the right offer is pivotal for aligning with your business objectives. The Azure OpenAI Service provides two distinct offerings: Standard and Provisioned. The Standard offering is optimized for low to medium volume workloads and offers flexibility with Pay-per-call pricing. This flexible, usage-based model with variable costs and capped throughput, is ideal for development, testing, and lower-volume production workloads.On the other hand, the Provisioned offering is designed for high volume workloads and throughput-heavy applications, offering predictable costs with hourly billing and the option for Azure Reservations. It offers a consistent cost structure and scalable throughput, suitable for production and high-volume workloads that need guaranteed latency.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.Serverless Model DeploymentGone are the days of cumbersome model deployment processes. With Azure AI Foundry’s model deployment, you can seamlessly deploy models through Models-as-a-Service (MaaS) or Models-as-a-Platform (MaaP). MaaS lets you fine-tune models without provisioning compute, making it easier for generative AI developers to build large language model (LLM) applications, while MaaP lets you innovate with models from vetted providers using the generative AI model store.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: Building intelligent apps with Azure AI services unlocks a wealth of capabilities for developers to create powerful and versatile applications.Azure OpenAI Service provides access to robust AI models while ensuring scalability, compliance, and security, all seamlessly integrated with other Azure services.Azure AI Search offers AI enrichment, semantic ranking, and vector search functionalities, enabling advanced content organization and retrieval.Azure AI Speech empowers apps with speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and speaker recognition features, enhancing accessibility and user interaction.Azure AI Vision facilitates image and face analysis, custom model training, and document text extraction, enriching apps with powerful visual capabilities.Azure AI Content Safety ensures enhanced safety through AI-driven content moderation and Jailbreak Risk prevention, while Azure AI Document Intelligence enables automated documentation generation and quality analysis, enhancing documentation experiences.Additionally, Azure AI Language and Azure AI Translator support customized translation, multilingual comprehension, and highly-accurate text and speech translations, making apps accessible and user-friendly across diverse audiences and industries.

slides.title: Azure OpenAI Service (AOAI)
slides.notes: Azure OpenAI Service is designed to enable developers and businesses to utilize cutting-edge AI models, such as GPT, for a wide range of applications, without the need for deep AI knowledge or significant investments in infrastructure. By combining OpenAI's advanced AI technologies with Azure's robust cloud platform, users can create and scale innovative AI-driven solutions across different sectors efficiently. Tapping into OpenAI's powerful models like GPT and DALL-E 3, coupled with Azure's cloud platform, users can create innovative AI-driven solutions across various industries. With features such as Whisper, users can transcribe and translate audio across multiple languages with ease. Azure OpenAI Service also facilitates seamless integration with Azure services, enhancing AI capabilities within existing applications. Moreover, users can customize and fine-tune AI models to meet specific use cases while ensuring compliance and security standards for trustworthy deploymentsOther benefits include:Access advanced AI models like GPT for diverse applications and tasks.Generate high-quality imagery with DALL-E 3 through textual descriptions and text-promptTranscribe and translate audio with Whisper, converting 30-second segments into text across 57 languagesIntegrate seamlessly with Azure services, enhancing AI capabilities and workflows within existing applications.Customize and fine-tune AI models to suit specific use cases and requirements effectively.Ensure compliance and security standards, adhering to responsible AI practices and regulations for trustworthy deployments.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: Azure AI Agent service: So why do agents matter? LLMs have amazing generative super-powers, but have your tried to ask an LLM what time is it? Or the weather? Or something that it’s not trained on? It will disappoint you!To build a more realistic application you don’t just need to be able to query data, call apis, or even execute code, but you also need to know when. This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Slide 11
slides.notes: With a suite of powerful, complete AI toolchain capabilities, developers can seamlessly integrate AI into their workflows, streamline development processes, and ensure optimal performance every step of the way.Ground Models on Your Protected DataEmpower your AI initiatives by grounding models on your data with Azure AI Foundry’s advanced data integration and model fine-tuning capabilities. Connect models directly to your data sources, enabling deeper insights and more accurate predictions. With the ability to fine-tune models based on your unique datasets, you can unlock hidden patterns and optimize performance like never before.Orchestrateand Debug AI WorkflowsStreamline AI application development with Azure AI Studio's intuitive orchestration tools. Whether you prefer prompt-based interactions, visual workflows, or code-first experiences, our platform offers the flexibility and ease of use you need to bring your ideas to life. From simplifying complex workflows to enabling interactive experimentation with variants, our orchestration capabilities empower developers to unleash their creativity and drive innovation at scale.Assess and Debug Your AppEnsure the success of your AI applications with Azure AI Foundry’s robust evaluation and training capabilities. Evaluate models and responses to fine-tune performance and align with your business needs before deploying to production. With comprehensive performance metrics and insights, you can make informed decisions to optimize your AI solutions for maximum impact. By rigorously evaluating, training, and debugging your models, you can confidently deploy them into production environments, knowing they'll deliver exceptional results.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 14
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry brings together all the elements developers need to harness the potential of AI into a unified, user-friendly, code-first platform.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production.With your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 6
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog now offers nearly 1,600 foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models direct from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI deployments, choosing the right offer is pivotal for aligning with your business objectives. The Azure OpenAI Service provides two distinct offerings: Standard and Provisioned. The Standard offering is optimized for low to medium volume workloads and offers flexibility with Pay-per-call pricing. This flexible, usage-based model with variable costs and capped throughput, is ideal for development, testing, and lower-volume production workloads.On the other hand, the Provisioned offering is designed for high volume workloads and throughput-heavy applications, offering predictable costs with hourly billing and the option for Azure Reservations. It offers a consistent cost structure and scalable throughput, suitable for production and high-volume workloads that need guaranteed latency.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.Serverless Model DeploymentGone are the days of cumbersome model deployment processes. With Azure AI Foundry’s model deployment, you can seamlessly deploy models through Models-as-a-Service (MaaS) or Models-as-a-Platform (MaaP). MaaS lets you fine-tune models without provisioning compute, making it easier for generative AI developers to build large language model (LLM) applications, while MaaP lets you innovate with models from vetted providers using the generative AI model store.

For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 14
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production.With your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog now offers nearly 1,600 foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI deployments, choosing the right offer is pivotal for aligning with your business objectives. The Azure OpenAI Service provides two distinct offerings: Standard and Provisioned. The Standard offering is optimized for low to medium volume workloads and offers flexibility with Pay-per-call pricing. This flexible, usage-based model with variable costs and capped throughput, is ideal for development, testing, and lower-volume production workloads.On the other hand, the Provisioned offering is designed for high volume workloads and throughput-heavy applications, offering predictable costs with hourly billing and the option for Azure Reservations. It offers a consistent cost structure and scalable throughput, suitable for production and high-volume workloads that need guaranteed latency.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.Serverless Model DeploymentGone are the days of cumbersome model deployment processes. With Azure AI Foundry’s model deployment, you can seamlessly deploy models through Models-as-a-Service (MaaS) or Models-as-a-Platform (MaaP). MaaS lets you fine-tune models without provisioning compute, making it easier for generative AI developers to build large language model (LLM) applications, while MaaP lets you innovate with models from vetted providers using the generative AI model store.

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.notes: Building intelligent apps with Azure AI services unlocks a wealth of capabilities for developers to create powerful and versatile applications.Azure OpenAI Service provides access to robust AI models while ensuring scalability, compliance, and security, all seamlessly integrated with other Azure services.Azure AI Search offers AI enrichment, semantic ranking, and vector search functionalities, enabling advanced content organization and retrieval.Azure AI Speech empowers apps with speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and speaker recognition features, enhancing accessibility and user interaction.Azure AI Vision facilitates image and face analysis, custom model training, and document text extraction, enriching apps with powerful visual capabilities.Azure AI Content Safety ensures enhanced safety through AI-driven content moderation and Jailbreak Risk prevention, while Azure AI Document Intelligence enables automated documentation generation and quality analysis, enhancing documentation experiences.Additionally, Azure AI Language and Azure AI Translator support customized translation, multilingual comprehension, and highly-accurate text and speech translations, making apps accessible and user-friendly across diverse audiences and industries.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: Azure AI Agent service: So why do agents matter? LLMs have amazing generative super-powers, but have your tried to ask an LLM what time is it? Or the weather? Or something that it’s not trained on? It will disappoint you!To build a more realistic application you don’t just need to be able to query data, call apis, or even execute code, but you also need to know when. This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: With a suite of powerful, complete AI toolchain capabilities, developers can seamlessly integrate AI into their workflows, streamline development processes, and ensure optimal performance every step of the way.Ground Models on Your Protected DataEmpower your AI initiatives by grounding models on your data with Azure AI Foundry’s advanced data integration and model fine-tuning capabilities. Connect models directly to your data sources, enabling deeper insights and more accurate predictions. With the ability to fine-tune models based on your unique datasets, you can unlock hidden patterns and optimize performance like never before.Orchestrateand Debug AI WorkflowsStreamline AI application development with Azure AI Foundry's intuitive orchestration tools. Whether you prefer prompt-based interactions, visual workflows, or code-first experiences, our platform offers the flexibility and ease of use you need to bring your ideas to life. From simplifying complex workflows to enabling interactive experimentation with variants, our orchestration capabilities empower developers to unleash their creativity and drive innovation at scale.Assess and Debug Your AppEnsure the success of your AI applications with Azure AI Foundry’s robust evaluation and training capabilities. Evaluate models and responses to fine-tune performance and align with your business needs before deploying to production. With comprehensive performance metrics and insights, you can make informed decisions to optimize your AI solutions for maximum impact. By rigorously evaluating, training, and debugging your models, you can confidently deploy them into production environments, knowing they'll deliver exceptional results.

Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: With a suite of powerful, complete AI toolchain capabilities, developers can seamlessly integrate AI into their workflows, streamline development processes, and ensure optimal performance every step of the way.Ground Models on Your Protected DataEmpower your AI initiatives by grounding models on your data with Azure AI Foundry’s advanced data integration and model fine-tuning capabilities. Connect models directly to your data sources, enabling deeper insights and more accurate predictions. With the ability to fine-tune models based on your unique datasets, you can unlock hidden patterns and optimize performance like never before.Orchestrateand Debug AI WorkflowsStreamline AI application development with Azure AI Foundry's intuitive orchestration tools. Whether you prefer prompt-based interactions, visual workflows, or code-first experiences, our platform offers the flexibility and ease of use you need to bring your ideas to life. From simplifying complex workflows to enabling interactive experimentation with variants, our orchestration capabilities empower developers to unleash their creativity and drive innovation at scale.Assess and Debug Your AppEnsure the success of your AI applications with Azure AI Foundry’s robust evaluation and training capabilities. Evaluate models and responses to fine-tune performance and align with your business needs before deploying to production. With comprehensive performance metrics and insights, you can make informed decisions to optimize your AI solutions for maximum impact. By rigorously evaluating, training, and debugging your models, you can confidently deploy them into production environments, knowing they'll deliver exceptional results.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.
