slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production.With your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog now offers nearly 1,600 foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI deployments, choosing the right offer is pivotal for aligning with your business objectives. The Azure OpenAI Service provides two distinct offerings: Standard and Provisioned. The Standard offering is optimized for low to medium volume workloads and offers flexibility with Pay-per-call pricing. This flexible, usage-based model with variable costs and capped throughput, is ideal for development, testing, and lower-volume production workloads.On the other hand, the Provisioned offering is designed for high volume workloads and throughput-heavy applications, offering predictable costs with hourly billing and the option for Azure Reservations. It offers a consistent cost structure and scalable throughput, suitable for production and high-volume workloads that need guaranteed latency.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.Serverless Model DeploymentGone are the days of cumbersome model deployment processes. With Azure AI Foundry’s model deployment, you can seamlessly deploy models through Models-as-a-Service (MaaS) or Models-as-a-Platform (MaaP). MaaS lets you fine-tune models without provisioning compute, making it easier for generative AI developers to build large language model (LLM) applications, while MaaP lets you innovate with models from vetted providers using the generative AI model store.

slides.notes: Building intelligent apps with Azure AI services unlocks a wealth of capabilities for developers to create powerful and versatile applications.Azure OpenAI Service provides access to robust AI models while ensuring scalability, compliance, and security, all seamlessly integrated with other Azure services.Azure AI Search offers AI enrichment, semantic ranking, and vector search functionalities, enabling advanced content organization and retrieval.Azure AI Speech empowers apps with speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and speaker recognition features, enhancing accessibility and user interaction.Azure AI Vision facilitates image and face analysis, custom model training, and document text extraction, enriching apps with powerful visual capabilities.Azure AI Content Safety ensures enhanced safety through AI-driven content moderation and Jailbreak Risk prevention, while Azure AI Document Intelligence enables automated documentation generation and quality analysis, enhancing documentation experiences.Additionally, Azure AI Language and Azure AI Translator support customized translation, multilingual comprehension, and highly-accurate text and speech translations, making apps accessible and user-friendly across diverse audiences and industries.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: Azure AI Agent service: So why do agents matter? LLMs have amazing generative super-powers, but have your tried to ask an LLM what time is it? Or the weather? Or something that it’s not trained on? It will disappoint you!To build a more realistic application you don’t just need to be able to query data, call apis, or even execute code, but you also need to know when. This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: With a suite of powerful, complete AI toolchain capabilities, developers can seamlessly integrate AI into their workflows, streamline development processes, and ensure optimal performance every step of the way.Ground Models on Your Protected DataEmpower your AI initiatives by grounding models on your data with Azure AI Foundry’s advanced data integration and model fine-tuning capabilities. Connect models directly to your data sources, enabling deeper insights and more accurate predictions. With the ability to fine-tune models based on your unique datasets, you can unlock hidden patterns and optimize performance like never before.Orchestrateand Debug AI WorkflowsStreamline AI application development with Azure AI Foundry's intuitive orchestration tools. Whether you prefer prompt-based interactions, visual workflows, or code-first experiences, our platform offers the flexibility and ease of use you need to bring your ideas to life. From simplifying complex workflows to enabling interactive experimentation with variants, our orchestration capabilities empower developers to unleash their creativity and drive innovation at scale.Assess and Debug Your AppEnsure the success of your AI applications with Azure AI Foundry’s robust evaluation and training capabilities. Evaluate models and responses to fine-tune performance and align with your business needs before deploying to production. With comprehensive performance metrics and insights, you can make informed decisions to optimize your AI solutions for maximum impact. By rigorously evaluating, training, and debugging your models, you can confidently deploy them into production environments, knowing they'll deliver exceptional results.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 12
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production. Azure AI Foundry integrates with Azure Arc to extend AI workloads across cloud, hybrid, and edge environments with unified governance, security, and deployment. With Foundry Local, organizations can also run models and agents directly on local machines, enabling offline execution with lower latency, stronger data privacy, and cost efficiency while staying connected to the Foundry ecosystemWith your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog offers foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI model deployments, selecting the right configuration is key to optimizing performance and cost. Azure AI Foundry offers three deployment options: Standard, ideal for flexible, general-purpose workloads with shared infrastructure; Provisioned, designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications with reserved capacity and predictable performance; and Batch, best suited for asynchronous, large-scale processing tasks like bulk summarization, offering cost-efficient parallel execution.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.

Azure AI Foundry offers three deployment options: Standard, ideal for flexible, general-purpose workloads with shared infrastructure; Provisioned, designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications with reserved capacity and predictable performance; and Batch, best suited for asynchronous, large-scale processing tasks like bulk summarization, offering cost-efficient parallel execution.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.notes: Building intelligent apps with Azure AI services unlocks a wealth of capabilities for developers to create powerful and versatile applications.Azure OpenAI Service provides access to robust AI models while ensuring scalability, compliance, and security, all seamlessly integrated with other Azure services.Azure AI Search offers AI enrichment, semantic ranking, and vector search functionalities, enabling advanced content organization and retrieval.Azure AI Speech empowers apps with speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and speaker recognition features, enhancing accessibility and user interaction.Azure AI Vision facilitates image and face analysis, custom model training, and document text extraction, enriching apps with powerful visual capabilities.Azure AI Content Safety ensures enhanced safety through AI-driven content moderation and Jailbreak Risk prevention, while Azure AI Document Intelligence enables automated documentation generation and quality analysis, enhancing documentation experiences.Additionally, Azure AI Language and Azure AI Translator support customized translation, multilingual comprehension, and highly-accurate text and speech translations, making apps accessible and user-friendly across diverse audiences and industries.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: Azure AI Agent service: So why do agents matter? LLMs have amazing generative super-powers, but have your tried to ask an LLM what time is it? Or the weather? Or something that it’s not trained on? It will disappoint you!To build a more realistic application you don’t just need to be able to query data, call apis, or even execute code, but you also need to know when. This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: To summarize, Azure AI Foundry brings together all the elements developers need to harness the potential of AI into a unified, user-friendly, code-first platform. With a suite of powerful, complete AI toolchain capabilities, developers can seamlessly integrate AI into their workflows, streamline development processes, and ensure optimal performance every step of the way.Ground Models on Your Protected DataEmpower your AI initiatives by grounding models on your data with Azure AI Foundry’s advanced data integration and model fine-tuning capabilities. Connect models directly to your data sources, enabling deeper insights and more accurate predictions. With the ability to fine-tune models based on your unique datasets, you can unlock hidden patterns and optimize performance like never before.Orchestrateand Debug AI WorkflowsStreamline AI application development with Azure AI Foundry's intuitive orchestration tools. Whether you prefer prompt-based interactions, visual workflows, or code-first experiences, our platform offers the flexibility and ease of use you need to bring your ideas to life. From simplifying complex workflows to enabling interactive experimentation with variants, our orchestration capabilities empower developers to unleash their creativity and drive innovation at scale.Assess and Debug Your AppEnsure the success of your AI applications with Azure AI Foundry’s robust evaluation and training capabilities. Evaluate models and responses to fine-tune performance and align with your business needs before deploying to production. With comprehensive performance metrics and insights, you can make informed decisions to optimize your AI solutions for maximum impact. By rigorously evaluating, training, and debugging your models, you can confidently deploy them into production environments, knowing they'll deliver exceptional results.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 12
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production. Azure AI Foundry integrates with Azure Arc to extend AI workloads across cloud, hybrid, and edge environments with unified governance, security, and deployment. With Foundry Local, organizations can also run models and agents directly on local machines, enabling offline execution with lower latency, stronger data privacy, and cost efficiency while staying connected to the Foundry ecosystemWith your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog offers foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 11
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production. Azure AI Foundry integrates with Azure Arc to extend AI workloads across cloud, hybrid, and edge environments with unified governance, security, and deployment. With Foundry Local, organizations can also run models and agents directly on local machines, enabling offline execution with lower latency, stronger data privacy, and cost efficiency while staying connected to the Foundry ecosystemWith your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog offers foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI model deployments, selecting the right configuration is key to optimizing performance and cost. Azure AI Foundry offers three deployment options: Standard, ideal for flexible, general-purpose workloads with shared infrastructure; Provisioned, designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications with reserved capacity and predictable performance; and Batch, best suited for asynchronous, large-scale processing tasks like bulk summarization, offering cost-efficient parallel execution.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.

slides.title: Power your digital transformationwith Microsoft for Healthcare | Pharma
slides.notes: Today we’ll explore how pharma companies can power their digital transformation with Microsoft. We’ll start with industry trends and challenges, then walk through how Microsoft can help accelerate research, empower your workforce, enhance patient and provider experiences, operate with agility, and safeguard data and infrastructure. Finally, we’ll touch on trust and next steps.

slides.title: Healthcare’s rapid evolution is raising challenges for Pharma
slides.notes: Our mission in Healthcare is to help solve some of the biggest challenges for pharma organizations that are emerging out of today’s rapid industry evolution. Over the last few years, healthcare organizations have been burdened by an unprecedented volume of challenging moments that have forced many to rethink established norms and operational best practices. Developing a new drug costs $1–2.6 Billion and takes 10–15 years from discovery to market153% of companies generating more than $1 Billion in revenue are reporting that silos derail efficiency2Less than 20% of pharmaceutical executives have reported successfully scaling digital and AI transformation3Pharma organizations are experiencing these challenges while also facing pressure to remain secure and compliant with ever-changing regulations. Our commitment has always been to ensure the tools we provide are up to the task of supporting our customers in their time of need. We know that technology has a role to play in accelerating progress for pressing healthcare industry concerns.

slides.title: Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation
slides.notes: Additionally, Pharma companies face unique trends impacting their value creation:Demonstrating product efficacy as medtech companies produce a majority of health data (such as imaging or telemetry), putting pressure on med device companies to optimize products for success in value-based care models. Both med device and medtech companies are looking to develop new digital products to monetize data, algorithms, and workflows, thus changing their role in healthcare (from supplier/enabler to active participant) and the broader life sciences ecosystem. This is also an opportunity to increase brand/franchise affinity.Due to cost sensitivity, customers are frequently opting for new pricing structures and options to bundle medical devices and technologies, vs. traditional buying behaviors of stand-alone purchases, putting pressure on medical device companies to rethink their pricing model.Dashboards are often populated with non-real time data, making it challenging to visualize patient data to execute decisions for better care.Disparity between medical device demand and production, as demand for medical devices have increased and device manufacturers are often unable to get the materials they need to meet that demand.Demonstrating efficacy of medical devices to payors. With the shift to value-based care, many medical devices exist outside traditional insurance coverage because efficacy is not clearly proven.Changing, stringent regulations & long, expensive, and cumbersome FDA evaluation and approval process makes it difficult to seek approval for new developments, discouraging innovation, even as innovation becomes more essential to differentiate in a crowded market.

slides.title: Microsoft is helping Pharma companies like you achieve more
slides.notes: With tools infused across the entire healthcare ecosystem and now with the help of integrated AI, Microsoft is committed to helping you achieve more. We see exciting opportunities to:Accelerate research, discovery, and developmentEmpower your workforceEnhance patient and provider experiencesOperate with agility and resiliency Safeguard your people, health data, and infrastructure

slides.title: Help enhance R&D and speed up development from clinical trial planning to submission
slides.notes: Accelerate your R&D teams with AI-enabled tools that enhance productivity and modernize discovery, design, and regulatory processes to transform your research and development. Create a “lab of the future” with seamless data sharing and remote collaboration to empower researchers with insights. Through clinical trial strategy and execution including decentralized trials, bring drugs to market faster. Using tools like: Healthcare data solutions in Microsoft FabricAzure Health Data & AI ServicesHealthcare AI models in Azure AI FoundryMicrosoft 365 CopilotHealthcare agent services in Copilot StudioMicrosoft Security Healthcare application templates in Microsoft Purview Dynamics 365

slides.title: Empower your team with integrated AI tools and automation, designed to accelerate productivity all within a trusted, compliant environment
slides.notes: Leverage AI to power teams and to meet growing demands for high-quality content and customer support. Provide your teams with the ability to meet changing demand by offering efficient and convenient multi-channels tools and safeguarded data transparency they need for more productivity and enhanced customer engagement and performance. Using tools like: Azure Health Data & AI ServicesPower PlatformMicrosoft 365 CopilotHealthcare agent services in Copilot StudioDynamics 365

slides.title: Enable continuous drug monitoring, generating actionable insights that enhance patient care and outcomes
slides.notes: Provide an accessible and high-quality experience to support personalized engagement, education, and adherence and demonstrate real-world impact. Tailor learning experiences and tools to cater to individual preferences. Support better direct-to-patient and provider-to-patient interactions. Optimize omnichannel communication and engagement tools to personalize interactions across platforms. Using tools like: Healthcare data solutions in Microsoft FabricAzure Health Data & AI ServicesDynamics 365 Healthcare AI models in Azure AI FoundryMicrosoft Security

slides.title: Operate with agility and resiliency
slides.notes: Operate with agility and resiliency by: Optimize business operations with unified data: Increase operational insights to accelerate product delivery and reduce manual work.Transform R&D with digital engineering: Create digital representations of your physical products, assets, and factories. Accelerate product design and R&D to realize productivity and efficiency savings, reducing cycle times and time-to-market.Modernize plant operations with AI innovation: Accelerate modern and innovative AI powered manufacturing of the future to support compliant, safe, enhanced security, and sustainable production through migration with reliable quality and yield, optimize resource utilization and asset maintenance with industrial IoT, data, and AI agents.Augmented end to end supply chain management: Leverage predictive and prescriptive planning with intelligent scheduling and metrics tracking for lead time reduction.  Enhance autonomous warehousing and logistics with forecasting and regulatory stock transparency and end to end visibility. Unlock supply chain intelligence while supporting regulated supply chain compliance.

slides.title: Healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
slides.notes: The release for healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric includes the following solutions/capabilities:FHIR data ingestion: Bring your Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) data to OneLake from a FHIR service such as Azure Health Data Services. OneLake, in Microsoft Fabric, is a unified, logical data lake that is designed to be a single place for all your analytics data.Healthcare data foundations: Set up your healthcare data estate quickly with FHIR data standards and ready-to-run data pipelines that are designed to efficiently structure data for analytics and AI/machine learning modeling..Unstructured clinical notes enrichment: Utilize Azure AI Language's Text Analytics for health services to extract and add structure to unstructured clinical notes for analytics. OMOP analytics: Prepare data for standardized analytics through Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership (OMOP) open community standards. Dynamics 365 Customer Insights - Data preparation: Connect Dynamics 365 Customer Insights to your OneLake on Fabric for creating patient or member lists for outreach.

slides.title: Azure AI Services for Health
slides.notes: Here is how Azure AI services for health can aid your business through a more healthcare-focused lens.Through Azure, pre-built healthcare AI models enable customers to build their own AI-based applications in the era of GenAI.Here is some information about this service:Azure AI Services for Health leverage Microsoft’s breadth of industry-agnostic AI capabilities. These services are specifically designed for the healthcare domain.Azure AI Services for Health bring pre-built AI tools to developers and data scientists, streamlining the integration of artificial intelligence into health-related applications.These services are created with health-specific models, aiming to enable a variety of use cases within the healthcare industry.The platform extends horizontal services while also adding domain-specific ones where needed, ensuring a tailored approach to healthcare challenges.Customers of all skill levels can easily incorporate AI capabilities into their applications, democratizing access to cutting-edge technology.These services are available via API calls, allowing developers to embed functionalities that enable applications to see, hear, speak, search, understand complex data, and accelerate advanced decision-making.Some Azure AI Services for Health are built on the Cognitive Services platform, while others are standalone offerings designed specifically for healthcare applications.Here are some more specific features that Azure AI services for health can provide, in combination with Azure AI Health Bot with generative AI technology.-Text Analytics for health, Clinical Data Classification, DE-ID, and Medical Vision as a Service Vision for HealthAdditionally, Azure AI services enables:-Azure AI Bot Service, Azure Open AI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure Form Recognizer, Azure Video Analyzer, Azure AI Vision, Azure AI Speech, Azure AI Language

This is also an opportunity to increase brand/franchise affinity.Due to cost sensitivity, customers are frequently opting for new pricing structures and options to bundle medical devices and technologies, vs. traditional buying behaviors of stand-alone purchases, putting pressure on medical device companies to rethink their pricing model.Dashboards are often populated with non-real time data, making it challenging to visualize patient data to execute decisions for better care.Disparity between medical device demand and production, as demand for medical devices have increased and device manufacturers are often unable to get the materials they need to meet that demand.Demonstrating efficacy of medical devices to payors. With the shift to value-based care, many medical devices exist outside traditional insurance coverage because efficacy is not clearly proven.Changing, stringent regulations & long, expensive, and cumbersome FDA evaluation and approval process makes it difficult to seek approval for new developments, discouraging innovation, even as innovation becomes more essential to differentiate in a crowded market.

slides.title: Healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric
slides.notes: The release for healthcare data solutions in Microsoft Fabric includes the following solutions/capabilities:FHIR data ingestion: Bring your Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) data to OneLake from a FHIR service such as Azure Health Data Services. OneLake, in Microsoft Fabric, is a unified, logical data lake that is designed to be a single place for all your analytics data.Healthcare data foundations: Set up your healthcare data estate quickly with FHIR data standards and ready-to-run data pipelines that are designed to efficiently structure data for analytics and AI/machine learning modeling..Unstructured clinical notes enrichment: Utilize Azure AI Language's Text Analytics for health services to extract and add structure to unstructured clinical notes for analytics. OMOP analytics: Prepare data for standardized analytics through Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership (OMOP) open community standards. Dynamics 365 Customer Insights - Data preparation: Connect Dynamics 365 Customer Insights to your OneLake on Fabric for creating patient or member lists for outreach.

slides.title: Azure AI Services for Health
slides.notes: Here is how Azure AI services for health can aid your business through a more healthcare-focused lens.Through Azure, pre-built healthcare AI models enable customers to build their own AI-based applications in the era of GenAI.Here is some information about this service:Azure AI Services for Health leverage Microsoft’s breadth of industry-agnostic AI capabilities. These services are specifically designed for the healthcare domain.Azure AI Services for Health bring pre-built AI tools to developers and data scientists, streamlining the integration of artificial intelligence into health-related applications.These services are created with health-specific models, aiming to enable a variety of use cases within the healthcare industry.The platform extends horizontal services while also adding domain-specific ones where needed, ensuring a tailored approach to healthcare challenges.Customers of all skill levels can easily incorporate AI capabilities into their applications, democratizing access to cutting-edge technology.These services are available via API calls, allowing developers to embed functionalities that enable applications to see, hear, speak, search, understand complex data, and accelerate advanced decision-making.Some Azure AI Services for Health are built on the Cognitive Services platform, while others are standalone offerings designed specifically for healthcare applications.Here are some more specific features that Azure AI services for health can provide, in combination with Azure AI Health Bot with generative AI technology.-Text Analytics for health, Clinical Data Classification, DE-ID, and Medical Vision as a Service Vision for HealthAdditionally, Azure AI services enables:-Azure AI Bot Service, Azure Open AI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure Form Recognizer, Azure Video Analyzer, Azure AI Vision, Azure AI Speech, Azure AI Language

slides.notes: Thank you

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production. Azure AI Foundry integrates with Azure Arc to extend AI workloads across cloud, hybrid, and edge environments with unified governance, security, and deployment. With Foundry Local, organizations can also run models and agents directly on local machines, enabling offline execution with lower latency, stronger data privacy, and cost efficiency while staying connected to the Foundry ecosystemWith your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog offers foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI model deployments, selecting the right configuration is key to optimizing performance and cost. Azure AI Foundry offers three deployment options: Standard, ideal for flexible, general-purpose workloads with shared infrastructure; Provisioned, designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications with reserved capacity and predictable performance; and Batch, best suited for asynchronous, large-scale processing tasks like bulk summarization, offering cost-efficient parallel execution.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.notes: Building intelligent apps with Azure AI services unlocks a wealth of capabilities for developers to create powerful and versatile applications.Azure OpenAI Service provides access to robust AI models while ensuring scalability, compliance, and security, all seamlessly integrated with other Azure services.Azure AI Search offers AI enrichment, semantic ranking, and vector search functionalities, enabling advanced content organization and retrieval.Azure AI Speech empowers apps with speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and speaker recognition features, enhancing accessibility and user interaction.Azure AI Vision facilitates image and face analysis, custom model training, and document text extraction, enriching apps with powerful visual capabilities.Azure AI Content Safety ensures enhanced safety through AI-driven content moderation and Jailbreak Risk prevention, while Azure AI Document Intelligence enables automated documentation generation and quality analysis, enhancing documentation experiences.Additionally, Azure AI Language and Azure AI Translator support customized translation, multilingual comprehension, and highly-accurate text and speech translations, making apps accessible and user-friendly across diverse audiences and industries.

slides.title: Slide 8
slides.notes: Azure AI Agent service: So why do agents matter? LLMs have amazing generative super-powers, but have your tried to ask an LLM what time is it? Or the weather? Or something that it’s not trained on? It will disappoint you!To build a more realistic application you don’t just need to be able to query data, call apis, or even execute code, but you also need to know when. This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 11
slides.notes: Thank You

This is where you can use an agent framework to build more complex tasks is useful.Rapidly develop and automate processes: Agents need to seamlessly integrate with the right tools, systems and APIs to perform deterministic or non-deterministic actions.Integrate with extensive memory and knowledge connectors: Agents need to manage conversation state and connect with internal and external knowledge sources to have the right context to complete a process.Flexible model choice: Agents built with the appropriate model for its task can enable better integration of information from multiple data types, yield better results for task-specific scenarios, and improve cost efficiencies in scaled agent deployments.Built-in enterprise readiness: Agents need to be able to support an organization's unique data privacy and compliance needs, scale with an organization's needs, and complete tasks reliably and with high quality.For ex. Code Interpreter answers questions by writing code, executing it, and giving a response. You can add functions to call external APIs or query data as we did here or input files for retrieval.

slides.title: Automated evaluation in Azure AI Foundry
slides.notes: Azure AI provides robust tools to help you systematically assess and improve your generative AI app before deploying to production. We support pre-built metrics for quality such as groundedness, relevance, and fluency. And now we also support pre-built metrics for Risk and Safety, so you can measure how susceptible your app is to generating problematic content, such as hateful or violent content, as well as how susceptible it is to direct jailbreak attacks.We’re also making it easier for customers to define their own custom metrics to run more tailored evaluations for their use case.In the context of generative AI, traditional ML metrics are useful when we want to quantify the accuracy of generated outputs compared to expected answers. For instance, in tasks such as classification or short-form question-answering, where there's typically one correct or expected answer, F1 scores can be used to measure the precision and recall of generated outputs against the expected answers.AI-assisted evaluations can be beneficial in scenarios where ground truth and expected answers aren’t available, such as open-ended question answering or creative writing, where single correct answers don‘t exist.

slides.title: Top use cases for
slides.notes: Wherever you focus your energy, the transformational experiences you can build have the potential to create incredible value. Here’s just a few top use cases where we’ve seen customers building intelligent apps of their own that leverage generative & agentic AI – and we have an incredible partner ecosystem that’s already innovating here that can help you as well.Chatbots and virtual assistants provide instant and accurate responses to customer queries – reducing wait times and improving overall operational efficiency.They can also analyze the questions to provide recommendations and offer personalized support in-the-moment or in follow up touch points, enhancing customer service, satisfaction and loyalty.With enterprise chat, organizations can securely and compliantly use natural language prompts to quickly and effectively knowledge mine or find the information they are looking for, ultimately improving productivity, job efficiency and satisfaction – all while reducing costs.Content generation can be time-consuming and resource-intensive. Generative AI algorithms automate the content creation process, allowing businesses to generate articles, blog posts, and other written and visual materials quickly, enabling organizations to save time while accelerating the pace of innovation.And in today’s world, we all know that personalization increases the chances of customer engagement and conversation and can significantly improve sales and marketing ROI. Generative AI enables businesses to deliver dynamic, hyper-personalized content tailored to individual preferences – optimizing engagement and conversation rates.

slides.title: Slide 11
slides.notes: Thank You

slides.title: Slide 1
slides.notes: AI is transforming every industry today — from reimagining how research and development happens, to empowering workforces, to creating entirely new customer experiences. We’re no longer just talking about pilots or experiments — organizations are moving fast toward real, enterprise-scale adoption.With that in mind, let us now dive into how Azure AI Foundry helps you design, customize, and manage AI apps and agents — bringing innovation to life in a secure, governed, and scalable way.

slides.title: Slide 2
slides.notes: Organizations today are facing challenges in developing AI apps & agents due to the limitations of current frameworks. These include a lack of integrated tools, security risks, and challenges in orchestrating complex workflows. What’s truly needed is a platform that connects complex workflow automation to enterprise data and seamlessly integrates with existing tools and applications. Flexible models must enable the processing of multiple types of data and modalities, and security must be built in to ensure compliance and data protection. The need is clear: organizations require platforms that make it easier to build and deploy scalable, secure AI agents rapidly.

slides.title: Slide 3
slides.notes: Microsoft offers three ways to build AI agents: GPUs & Containers for full control and customization, Azure AI Foundry for flexible orchestration with model choice and BYO storage, and Copilot Studio for fully-managed, low-code agent development. As you move from GPUs to Copilot Studio, ease of use increases while control and customization decrease.

slides.title: Slide 4
slides.notes: Azure AI Foundry is a trusted, integrated platform designed for Developers and IT Administrators to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents. It offers a rich set of AI capabilities and tools through a simple portal, unified SDK, and APIs to accelerate the path to production. What sets Azure AI Foundry apart is its accessibility through the world's most loved developer tools: GitHub, Visual Studio, and Copilot Studio. This integration enables developers to work within their preferred environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration. Key tools within Azure AI Foundry include Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Search, Azure AI Agent Service, and Azure AI Content Safety. These tools, combined with observability features like evaluations, customization, governance and monitoring, facilitate secure data integration, model customization, app orchestration, and experimentation with built-in trustworthy AI tools and principles. By unifying data, models, and operations into a single platform, Azure AI Foundry enables enterprises, start-ups and software developer companies to fully harness the potential of AI, accelerating innovation and optimizing application quality in production. Azure AI Foundry integrates with Azure Arc to extend AI workloads across cloud, hybrid, and edge environments with unified governance, security, and deployment. With Foundry Local, organizations can also run models and agents directly on local machines, enabling offline execution with lower latency, stronger data privacy, and cost efficiency while staying connected to the Foundry ecosystemWith your models, pipelines, and agents ready, it’s time to deploy. Every app and agent needs a runtime. We offer options from serverless (Azure Functions, Container Apps) to full control with AKS. Container Apps + Foundry: Deploy models directly from the Foundry catalog.Functions + Foundry: Build event-driven apps and agents with low overhead.Kubernetes: Easier deployment of open-source AI models across cloud and hybrid.

slides.title: Slide 5
slides.notes: In Azure AI Foundry We believe model choice is important. At this very moment, with thousands of models available. Whether you are seeking the most advanced reasoning, a computer use agent, an industry-based or domain-specific model, or a model suited for the task, Foundry has the models to help you get what you need done.With Azure AI Foundry, we bring a comprehensive selection of frontier and open models, enabling customers to choose the models that make most sense for their solutions. We have expanded our model collection by curating our own AI models and announcing new partnerships with industry-leading generative AI model providers. The Azure AI Foundry model catalog offers foundation models (LLMs and SLMs) from Databricks, Deci AI, Hugging Face, Meta, Microsoft Research, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Stability AI – and now Cohere – enabling Azure customers to choose the best model for their use case.  Unified Access: Seamlessly access Azure OpenAI, Microsoft models, partner-hosted models, and open-source LLMs in one place.Scalable Deployment: Deploy AI workloads faster with flexible options across serverless, managed, and provisioned compute.Enterprise-Ready: Built-in security, governance, and compliance features to meet enterprise AI requirements at scale.New models are joining Azure as direct, first-party offerings. These include Azure Llama, Azure Grok, Azure Mistral, and Azure Flux.

slides.title: Why choose Models directly from Azure?
slides.notes: In the realm of AI model deployments, selecting the right configuration is key to optimizing performance and cost. Azure AI Foundry offers three deployment options: Standard, ideal for flexible, general-purpose workloads with shared infrastructure; Provisioned, designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications with reserved capacity and predictable performance; and Batch, best suited for asynchronous, large-scale processing tasks like bulk summarization, offering cost-efficient parallel execution.As for deployment location. Global is best for cost-effective, broad model access; Data Zone suits cross-region load balancing and redundancy.Regional is ideal for localized data processing and compliance-sensitive workloads.
