slides.title: Work Trend Index 2025
slides.notes: Like years past, the WTI draws on a global survey, Microsoft 365 telemetry, and hiring and labor trends from LinkedIn. But new this year are insights from in-depth conversations with AI startups, economists, scientists, and academics.Taken together, these data and insights help us understand of how knowledge work is evolving—and what every company needs to do to adapt.

slides.title: The Frontier Firm
slides.notes: We are entering a new reality—one in which AI can reason and solve problems in remarkable ways.This on-tap intelligence will rewrite the rules of business and transform knowledge work as we know it.Organizations today must navigate the challenge of preparing for an AI-enhanced future. One in which AI agents will have increasing levels of capability over time that humans will need to harness as they redesign their organizations.Human ambition, creativity, and ingenuity will continue to create new economic value and opportunity as we redefine work and workflows. As a result, a new organizational blueprint is emerging, one that blends machine intelligence with human judgment, building systems that are AI-operated but human-led.As with the Industrial Revolution and the internet era, this transformation will take decades to show its full promise and involve broad technological, societal, and economic changes. Frontier Firms are already taking shape, and within the next 2–5 years we expect that every organization will be on their journey to becoming one. 82% of leaders say this is a pivotal year to rethink key aspects of strategy and operations.

slides.title: Journey to the Frontier Firm
slides.notes: Every company’s experience will be different, but we see this journey playing out in three phases: <click>In the first phase, AI acts as an assistant, helping people do the same work better and faster. <click>In phase 3, agents join teams as “digital colleagues,” taking on specific tasks at human direction—for instance, a researcher agent creating a go-to-market plan. <click>And in phase 3—people set direction for agents that run entire business processes and workflows, checking in as needed.Picture how a supply chain role may change: with agents handling end-to-end logistics, while humans guide the agent system, resolve exceptions, and manage supplier relationships. Human work isn’t replaced, it’s elevated.This won’t be a linear journey, and many companies will be in all three phases simultaneously..

slides.title: Human labor is reaching its limits
slides.notes: This is important because human labor is reaching its limits. 83% of employees & leaders globally say they don’t have enough time or energy to do their jobs, while 53% of leaders say productivity must rise.87% of leaders are confident in using agents to meet workforce capacity demands

slides.title: Most telecommunications leaders see agents as the way to scale
slides.notes: Our findings point to three key shifts that define a Frontier Firm:  First, intelligence is now on tap—giving business a new way to scale.Second, human–agent teams are upending the org chart.And third, every employee will soon become an Agent Boss—directing and managing agents to amplify their impact and accelerate their career.We aren’t running out of work—but we are running out of human capacity [workers can’t keep up with the pace/demands of productivity and interruptions, workers can’t focus on what matters] to do it. And leaders know this to be true. 81% of leaders are confident they’ll use AI agents to expand workforce capacity in the next 12-18 months..

slides.title: Telecommunications employees turn to AI for its unique strengths
slides.notes: This brings up an important question: with a new agentic workforce working alongside human employees, how will we organize and delegate tasks within companies?In short: we believe that human-agent teams will evolve the org chart into a work chart. Today, the org chart exists because companies are built around human expertise siloed in functions. But with expertise on demand, this may change.we don’t see this agentic workforce as substitutional. We see it as additional.And that is clear in the data as well. We asked our 31,000 respondents – why do you turn to AI over a human colleague? The share of employees who chose each option as their top reason: 24/7 availability, machine speed and quality and ideas on demand. These are all net-new qualities within an agentic workforce that humans cannot – and should not – provide.

slides.title: Al skilling and agents are top workforce strategies in Telecommunications
slides.notes: And finally, when agents join the team, every employee will need to become an agent boss.Imagine building your own agentic start-up within your company – a team of agents designed and customized to your needs.This mandates that all employees learn to manage, especially those just joining the workforce. Now I know there’s one important question that’s top of mind: If AI is doing more—what happens to people?So, we asked leaders directly. And here’s what they told us: 54% are prioritizing AI specific skillin, 41% are maintaining headcount but using AI as digital labor, 39% is investing in maintaining employee morale.The pressure is real. But that’s only half the story.

slides.title: New jobs emerging
slides.notes: New job roles are merging. These include roles like AI trainers, agent and data specialists, ROI analysts, and AI strategists in marketing, finance, and customer support

slides.title: In-demand skills for the new era of work
slides.notes: In a world of vast economic volatility – where 52% of employees say job security is no longer a given in their industry –preparing people for what’s next is a must.AI literacy is now the most in-demand skill of 2025, according to LinkedIn. Also rising are human strengths like conflict mitigation, adaptability, process automation, and innovative thinking—showing that the future belongs to those who can pair deep AI capabilities with the skills machines can’t replicate.

slides.title: Slide 10
slides.notes: At Microsoft, we believe that AI is a powerful tool to empower employees across organizations and create tangible value. To ensure your AI investments are truly impactful, we’ve developed something of a checklist of opportunities AI provides during an organizational transformation.First, your AI strategy should help enrich your employee’s experiences. A positive employee experience is crucial for attracting and retaining top talent, which in turn drives the next generation of growth for your company. AI can help create a more dynamic and fulfilling work environment, making your company more competitive in the talent market by –Enabling frontline workers to do their best work by unifying devices, data, relationships, and processes, into intelligent appsBy improving training and upskilling to enable productivity and efficiencySecond, consider how you are reinventing audience engagement through AI. Are you able to connect with more viewers at scale through better personalization? AI can help you understand your audience’s needs and preferences better, allowing you to offer tailored content and offerings. You can also enhance the connection with your audience by by –Modernizing customer experienceEnabling intelligent customer self-serviceThird, think about reshaping your business processes with AI. Traditional business processes are often artifacts of human workflows. By reimagining these processes with an AI-first approach, you can streamline operations and increase efficiency through task automation and streamlining production. Imagine having AI agents and Copilots working alongside your team, amplifying human ambition and enabling everyone to get more work done.Finally, ask yourself if you are bending the curve on innovation. Think about using AI to offload tedious, low-priority tasks, helping your talent think through more innovative stories or strategies to help develop movies, shows, and content faster. AI can accelerate your content creation cycle, helping you stay ahead of the competition and drive growth

slides.title: Thank you
slides.notes: It’s been a pleasure to be with you today. I hope that you have gain some insight on where we are heading, and learned some practical steps that will help you embrace the potential of AI.  I’m happy to share our research and learnings with you through this link. Thank you.

Frontier Firms are already taking shape, and within the next 2–5 years we expect that every organization will be on their journey to becoming one. 82% of leaders say this is a pivotal year to rethink key aspects of strategy and operations.

slides.title: Journey to the Frontier Firm
slides.notes: Every company’s experience will be different, but we see this journey playing out in three phases: <click>In the first phase, AI acts as an assistant, helping people do the same work better and faster. <click>In phase 3, agents join teams as “digital colleagues,” taking on specific tasks at human direction—for instance, a researcher agent creating a go-to-market plan. <click>And in phase 3—people set direction for agents that run entire business processes and workflows, checking in as needed.Picture how a supply chain role may change: with agents handling end-to-end logistics, while humans guide the agent system, resolve exceptions, and manage supplier relationships. Human work isn’t replaced, it’s elevated.This won’t be a linear journey, and many companies will be in all three phases simultaneously..

slides.title: Human labor is reaching its limits
slides.notes: This is important because human labor is reaching its limits. 83% of employees & leaders globally say they don’t have enough time or energy to do their jobs, while 53% of leaders say productivity must rise.87% of leaders are confident in using agents to meet workforce capacity demands

slides.title: Telecommunications employees turn to AI for its unique strengths
slides.notes: This brings up an important question: with a new agentic workforce working alongside human employees, how will we organize and delegate tasks within companies?In short: we believe that human-agent teams will evolve the org chart into a work chart. Today, the org chart exists because companies are built around human expertise siloed in functions. But with expertise on demand, this may change.we don’t see this agentic workforce as substitutional. We see it as additional.And that is clear in the data as well. We asked our 31,000 respondents – why do you turn to AI over a human colleague? The share of employees who chose each option as their top reason: 24/7 availability, machine speed and quality and ideas on demand. These are all net-new qualities within an agentic workforce that humans cannot – and should not – provide.

slides.title: In-demand skills for the new era of work
slides.notes: New job roles are merging. These include roles like AI trainers, agent and data specialists, ROI analysts, and AI strategists in marketing, finance, and customer supportAI literacy is now the most in-demand skill of 2025, according to LinkedIn. Also rising are human strengths like conflict mitigation, adaptability, process automation, and innovative thinking—showing that the future belongs to those who can pair deep AI capabilities with the skills machines can’t replicate.

slides.title: Slide 7
slides.notes: At Microsoft, we believe that AI is a powerful tool to empower employees across organizations and create tangible value. To ensure your AI investments are truly impactful, we’ve developed something of a checklist of opportunities AI provides during an organizational transformation.First, your AI strategy should help enrich your employee’s experiences. A positive employee experience is crucial for attracting and retaining top talent, which in turn drives the next generation of growth for your company. AI can help create a more dynamic and fulfilling work environment, making your company more competitive in the talent market by –Enabling frontline workers to do their best work by unifying devices, data, relationships, and processes, into intelligent appsBy improving training and upskilling to enable productivity and efficiencySecond, consider how you are reinventing audience engagement through AI. Are you able to connect with more viewers at scale through better personalization? AI can help you understand your audience’s needs and preferences better, allowing you to offer tailored content and offerings. You can also enhance the connection with your audience by by –Modernizing customer experienceEnabling intelligent customer self-serviceThird, think about reshaping your business processes with AI. Traditional business processes are often artifacts of human workflows. By reimagining these processes with an AI-first approach, you can streamline operations and increase efficiency through task automation and streamlining production. Imagine having AI agents and Copilots working alongside your team, amplifying human ambition and enabling everyone to get more work done.Finally, ask yourself if you are bending the curve on innovation. Think about using AI to offload tedious, low-priority tasks, helping your talent think through more innovative stories or strategies to help develop movies, shows, and content faster. AI can accelerate your content creation cycle, helping you stay ahead of the competition and drive growth

slides.title: Thank you
slides.notes: It’s been a pleasure to be with you today. I hope that you have gain some insight on where we are heading, and learned some practical steps that will help you embrace the potential of AI.  Thank you.

slides.title: Becoming Frontier | Retail
slides.notes: We see retail organizations that are Becoming Frontier as those that are using AI to enrich employee experiences, reinvent customer engagement, reshape business processes, and bend the curve on innovation. Enriching employee experiences - By empowering the retail workforce with AI tools to accelerate productivity and drive customer satisfactionReinventing customer engagement - By delighting customers across shopping touchpoints to boost conversionReshape business processes - By unlocking efficiency, visibility, and added workforce capacity for business growthBend the curve on innovation - By transforming the shopper experience with AI to increase engagement Next, we’ll explore great examples of how customers are making it real in their businesses with Microsoft. We’ll dive into their challenges, their approach and pathways to realizing value. And as we go through these examples, I encourage you to ask questions and share if any of the use cases resonate with your needs, desired ROI, or key benefits you’re looking to achieve.
